headline: Ford, GM EV Sales Plummet. What That Means for the Stocks.
summary: Thursday,  Ford Motor  reported first-quarter U.S. sales.  Fourth-quarter sales fell after buyers rushed to beat the September expiration of the $7,500 federal EV purchase tax credit.  First quarter hybrid electric vehicle sales were 51,073, down 20% from a year ago, accounting for 9% of total car sales, down 1 percentage point from a year ago.

headline: General Motors Resets EV Plans As Valuation Signals Potential Upside
summary: General Motors (NYSE:GM) is restructuring its manufacturing and EV plans, scaling back electric vehicle production at Factory ZERO. The company is temporarily laying off 1,300 workers at the Detroit plant while pausing certain EV operations. At the same time, GM is ramping up output of gas powered trucks and introducing new generative AI driven design processes. These moves reflect a broad operational reset across GM's vehicle mix and product development approach. For investors following...
